verifyCommand: uv run --extra vision pytest && uvx ruff check .
acceptanceCriteria: Verification passes (uv run --extra vision pytest && uvx ruff check .)
All pre-existing tests still pass unchanged
notes: Loop 3: a lender_concentration detector (GAO: a handful of nonbank/fintech lenders with auto-approval originated a disproportionate share of fraud-case loans). CRITICAL DESIGN — UNSUPERVISED & LABEL-FREE: the detector must NOT read fraud_cases / any labels (that would leak the answer and inherit prosecution bias — the deep-research warned about exactly this). Instead it is PEER-RELATIVE across lenders: for each originating_lender with enough loans, compute the lender's RATE of program-rule-suspicious loans (a label-free predicate, default 'amount_per_job >= the per-employee cap', reusing payroll_cap.py constants), robust-z that rate across lenders (median/MAD via stats.robust_z), and flag every loan from a lender in the extreme upper tail. The signal = 'this loan came from a lender whose whole book is unusually full of cap-busting loans'. CRITICAL METHODOLOGY (H6/Loop1/Loop2 lesson): register in registry.exploratory_detectors(), NOT all_detectors(); promotion is a MANUAL human decision after real-data lift validation, done AFTER this loop. GUARDRAILS: (1) NEVER run the relief-probe CLI / open data/relief_probe.duckdb; all tests seed a tmp_path warehouse via warehouse.connect(...). (2) No invented benchmark NUMBERS in docs — qualitative only. (3) Entity key loan_number (str). (4) REUSE: detectors/base.py (Detector/Signal), stats.py (robust_z, with min_mad), payroll_cap.py (per-employee cap constants $20,833 / $29,167 for NAICS '72'). Detector is READ-ONLY and must work on a warehouse with an EMPTY fraud_cases table (proving it's label-free). (5) Style: from __future__ import annotations, typed, docstrings, ruff line-length 90. Commit ONE feature per iteration; set passes=true; update plans/progress.md; re-read plans/guardrails.md each iteration.

features.title: lender_concentration detector (unsupervised, peer-relative, label-free)
acceptanceCriteria: New module src/relief_probe/detectors/lender_concentration.py defining LenderConcentrationDetector(Detector) with detector_id='lender_concentration' and a one-line summary

Groups loans by originating_lender; only considers lenders with >= min_loans loans (constructor param, documented default e.g. 100) so the rate is stable; for each such lender computes suspicious_rate = (# loans where amount_per_job = current_approval_amount/jobs_reported >= per-employee cap, jobs>=1) / (its loan count). The cap is the payroll_cap.py per-employee ceiling ($20,833; $29,167 for NAICS prefix '72') — a LABEL-FREE program rule

Robust-z the per-lender suspicious_rate across all qualifying lenders (stats.robust_z, optionally min_mad) and flag every loan from a lender whose z >= min_z (constructor param); score is the lender's z (or rate), comparable WITHIN this detector and monotonic in lender anomaly
Does NOT read fraud_cases or any labels; loans with null originating_lender or unusable jobs/amount are handled gracefully; read-only

tests/test_lender_concentration.py on a seeded tmp_path warehouse (with an EMPTY fraud_cases table, proving label-independence): a lender whose book is mostly cap-busting loans fires on ALL its loans; a clean peer lender stays quiet; lenders below min_loans are skipped; the detector returns results without ever querying fraud_cases
features.notes: The point is a LENDER-LEVEL signal: it flags even the individually-clean loans from a structurally-bad book, which per-loan detectors miss. Keep the suspicious predicate label-free and documented; peer-relative robust-z is the anomaly. Avoid raw volume (big banks legitimately have huge volume) — RATE is the metric.

features.title: Register lender_concentration as EXPLORATORY + integration
acceptanceCriteria: registry.exploratory_detectors() includes LenderConcentrationDetector(); all_detectors() is UNCHANGED (naics_cohort + payroll_cap + multiple_funded_loans) — NOT auto-promoted
get_detector('lender_concentration') resolves it; registry module docstring updated to list it (built + tested; pending real-data validation)

tests: 'lender_concentration' in exploratory_detectors() and NOT in all_detectors(); default run_all(con) omits it; run_all(con, detectors=[*all_detectors(), *exploratory_detectors()]) on a seeded warehouse includes its count
features.notes: Mirror the H6/Loop1/Loop2 disposition (SIGN-010). exploratory_detectors() now holds duplicate_address_ring, amount_anomaly, establishment_overcount, lender_concentration.

features.title: Docs: catalog lender_concentration (qualitative)
acceptanceCriteria: README detector catalog lists lender_concentration as an EXPLORATORY detector (built + tested, pending real-data validation), explains the UNSUPERVISED label-free peer-relative design (and why label-free: avoids prosecution-bias leakage), cites the GAO lender-concentration motivation, and states honest false-positive modes (a high cap-busting rate can reflect a lender's legitimate industry mix, e.g. NAICS-72-heavy books); NO benchmark/lift numbers
